response.justification: The candidate is a strong hands-on senior engineer with roughly 19 years of broad experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, which satisfies the minimum experience bar. However, this is a director-level IC role centered on AI strategy, and the resume shows career-long senior/staff execution roles rather than organization-wide technical leadership, strategic advisory work, or recognized AI expertise. His AI exposure (MCP code generators and agent tooling at Naver) is recent and infrastructure-focused, with no demonstrated AI-powered products or platforms, making him a weak fit for the role as scoped.
strengths: 19+ years of engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, embedded, and low-latency distributed systems
Recent hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work, including MCP code generators and agentic workflow tooling
Founder-level ownership of an open-source geo-data platform plus a track record of public talks and workshops
concerns: No evidence of director/principal-level scope, strategic influence, or advising customers and executives
AI experience is recent and tooling-oriented, with no recognized AI expertise or shipped AI products at scale
No advanced degree in an AI-related field and limited cloud platform strategy background

key_factors.factor: Lacks recognized AI expertise and AI product track record
Seniority gap versus director-level strategy role
Long, broad engineering experience meeting minimum bar
